patch.value:
GitHub: https://github.com/unbraind/pm-cli/issues/458

## Problem
`pm claim`/`pm start-task` reject --assignee (unknown_option) while pm update accepts it; agents naturally model claiming as assigning and try --assignee first. The recovery message lists where --assignee IS valid (create, list, list-all) but never points to --author, the actual claim-identity flag — a failed mutation in the critical path.

## Proposed fix
Either accept --assignee as an alias for --author on claim/release/start-task/pause-task (documented equivalence), or keep the contract strict and make the recovery message for these commands say: use --author <agent> for claim identity; use pm update --assignee for assignee metadata. Alias preferred per never-block routing precedent (2026-05-28 agent-UX pass).

## Definition of done
--assignee on lifecycle ownership commands either works or produces a targeted recovery hint naming --author; spec covers both commands; contracts/docs updated. Related: GH-444 item (global author ergonomics), pm-gdi7 (error suggestions).
GH-458: claim/start-task reject --assignee with an untargeted recovery hint (alias or better hint)

Dogfood GH-458: lifecycle ownership commands take --author while update takes --assignee; agents try --assignee first and the recovery message never mentions --author. Duplicate check: 2026-05-28 never-block pass predates this; no open overlap.
--assignee on claim/release/start-task/pause-task either aliases --author or yields a recovery hint naming --author and pm update --assignee; spec + contracts + docs updated
